name: simple_model
positioning: A repo-native Project Intelligence control plane for large, half-built projects: deterministic code facts, architectural intent, PR gates, work records, and agent slices that stay synchronized with real code rather than disposable agent memory.

implementation_audit.summary: v0.4 established executable surfaces for the full control plane, but most are thin deterministic MVPs. The next roadmap must turn those surfaces into production-grade project-maintenance capabilities: deeper language parsing, stable facts, real diff semantics, policy waivers, CI/PR integrations, and scalable incremental operation.
baseline_strengths: Multi-file struct resolution works and is integrated into bootstrap.
Repo ingest, adoption audit, interface scan, import scan, test scan, ownership, PR impact, PR gate, work records, MCP, federation, batch plan, and debt report all have runnable bash+jq entry points.
The suite has deterministic smoke coverage across the v0.4 surface.
The project direction is clearly differentiated: not another coding agent, but a repo-carried engineering fact layer.

major_gaps.gap: specs/code-facts.json is permissive and code_facts.sh mostly aggregates other reports. It lacks stable ids, source ranges, hashes, schema validation depth, and incremental cache semantics.

interface_scan.sh still extracts mostly names. It does not yet capture robust signatures, source spans, docstrings, type info, changed/removed interface comparison against a base ref, or per-interface hashes.

import_graph_scan.sh is best-effort regex and handles only simple relative imports. It does not resolve aliases, package roots, re-exports, monorepo workspaces, path aliases, Python packages, Go modules, or Rust crates.
test_surface_scan.sh finds test-looking files but does not reliably map tests to components via imports, coverage, package manifests, or declared check commands.

pr_impact.sh currently relies on exact file paths and pr_gate.sh bundles only shallow gate outputs. It lacks base/head diff handling, Markdown reports, waiver semantics, selected test execution, and stable exit taxonomy.
work_record_collect.sh validates only minimal fields and is not wired into dispatch, CBOM, PR gate, or summary collection as an audit ledger.
tools/simple_model_mcp.sh is a one-shot JSON-RPC wrapper, not a compliant multi-method MCP server with tool schemas, errors, streaming, or discovery.
federation_resolve.sh and batch_plan.sh are smoke-level. They do not resolve cross-repo component identities, downstream interfaces, owners, branch plans, or PR batch sizing.
The project can generate reports but lacks an adoption playbook, static dashboard, examples on real fixtures, and a guided path from unmanaged code to enforced gates.

implementation_audit.planning_principle: Do not add more thin surfaces until the existing control-plane surfaces become accurate, explainable, cacheable, and useful on real large repositories.

market_thesis.durable_gap: Most tools either execute coding tasks or infer repo maps. The durable gap is a continuously reconciled, repo-native engineering fact layer that can be consumed by any coding agent and enforced by CI.
differentiation: Deterministic facts and gates live in the repo, not in a hosted index or agent session.
Declared intent in struct fragments is reconciled with discovered code facts.
Work records, PR impact, interface drift, dependency drift, and CBOM provenance form an auditable maintenance loop.
Agents remain leaf workers; structural decisions remain deterministic.
market_thesis.north_star: A maintainer can run one command on an unfamiliar half-built repo and get: model coverage, public interfaces, dependency graph, tests, owners, PR impact, risk, selected checks, and gate verdicts.

production_readiness: A 100k+ LOC monorepo can run fact scans incrementally in under 60 seconds after the first scan.
PR impact maps changed files to components, public interfaces, tests, owners, and risk with useful precision.
Interface and dependency drift gates support explicit waivers and fail deterministically.
Agent dispatch writes work records that are linked into CBOM and PR gate output.
Reports are useful to humans without opening JSON by hand.
quality_bar: Every generator has JSON mode, human mode, stable exit codes, and tests.
Every schema has concrete required fields and validation fixtures.
Every production gate has at least one failure fixture and one adoption-mode waiver fixture.
No required runtime dependency beyond bash+jq; optional deeper parsers must be detected and reported as optional.

todos.task: Harden specs/code-facts.json into a strict schema with stable ids, source ranges, hashes, language, kind, visibility, component id, and provenance.
todos.current_gap: Current schema is permissive and does not enforce a useful fact contract.
todos.deliverable: Strict code facts schema plus validation tests.
acceptance: Schema rejects facts without id, kind, path, component, source hash, and provenance
Schema represents files, symbols, imports, exports, tests, routes, configs, owners, and checks
tests/test_code_facts_schema.sh covers valid and invalid fixtures

todos.task: Add content-hash based incremental scanning for code facts so unchanged files are reused from cache.
todos.current_gap: code_facts.sh recomputes everything and has no stable cache contract.
acceptance: First run scans all managed files and writes cache
Second run over unchanged repo reports cache hits
Changing one file invalidates only that file's facts
Cache key includes scanner version and file hash

todos.task: Upgrade interface_scan to capture best-effort signatures, source line ranges, symbol kind, visibility, and per-interface hashes across Python/TS/JS/Go/Rust.
todos.current_gap: Current scan mostly captures names and misses signature drift.
todos.deliverable: Signature-aware interface facts with golden fixtures.
acceptance: Captures function/class/type/interface names and signatures where regex can safely infer them
Captures start line for every discovered interface and end line when cheaply available
Computes interface_hash from normalized public surface
Golden fixtures cover Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust

todos.task: Compare public interfaces between base and head refs to classify added, removed, changed, and unchanged interfaces.
todos.current_gap: interface_drift_gate only compares current code to struct exports, not base/head interface changes.
acceptance: Accepts --base and --head git refs
Emits added/removed/changed interfaces by component
Uses interface_hash for changed detection
Works without modifying the worktree

todos.task: Replace simple import matching with language-aware path resolution for Python packages, TS path aliases/workspaces, Go modules, and Rust crates.
todos.current_gap: Current import scan only handles very simple relative imports.
acceptance: Reads package.json/tsconfig paths for TypeScript projects
Reads go.mod module roots
Reads Cargo.toml crate names and src layout
Handles Python relative imports and __init__.py packages
Reports unresolved imports separately from drift

todos.task: Map tests to components using import graph, naming conventions, co-location, declared checks, and package manifests.
todos.current_gap: Current test scan finds test files but does not map them with enough confidence.
acceptance: Each mapped test includes confidence and reason
Supports direct import, same-directory, naming, and declared check mapping
Reports ambiguous tests and orphan tests
Has fixtures for at least Python and TypeScript

todos.task: Improve ownership resolution with CODEOWNERS pattern matching, nearest-owner precedence, struct override rules, and optional git history hints.
todos.current_gap: Current ownership resolution uses only struct owners and a crude CODEOWNERS fallback.
acceptance: Parses CODEOWNERS patterns in order with last-match semantics
Struct component owner overrides module owner and CODEOWNERS
Reports ownership source for every owner
Optional git history mode is disabled by default and clearly marked

todos.task: Discover web/API routes, CLI commands, config files, env vars, database migrations, and feature flags as first-class code facts.
todos.current_gap: The model tracks symbols and imports but misses operational surfaces that often break production.
acceptance: Detects common route declarations in Express/FastAPI/Flask-like patterns using best-effort regex
Detects env var reads and config file references
Detects migration-looking files and links them to components by path
Outputs facts into code_facts.json

todos.task: Generate deterministic struct patch suggestions for unmanaged files, undeclared exports, undeclared imports, missing owners, and missing checks.
todos.current_gap: Reports identify drift but do not help maintainers update struct safely.
acceptance: Emits JSON Patch operations, not free-form text
Supports --apply only with explicit flag
Patch suggestions include source finding ids
Tests cover adding exports/imports/owners/checks

todos.task: Add a waiver model for adoption mode and intentional drift: owner, reason, expiry, scope, and finding id.
todos.current_gap: Current gates can only pass/fail/warn; large projects need controlled exceptions.
acceptance: Waivers can scope to finding id, component, path, or rule
Expired waivers fail
Waiver summary appears in gate output
Waivers are deterministic JSON files under .simple_model/waivers/

todos.task: Upgrade interface drift gate to support base/head interface diffs, downstream impact, and waivers.
todos.current_gap: Current interface gate does not detect changed signatures or manage intentional breaking changes.
acceptance: Fails on removed or changed exported interfaces unless waived
Warns on added public interfaces not declared in struct
Includes downstream impacted components from import graph
Outputs stable rule ids for waivers

todos.task: Upgrade dependency gate to enforce declared imports, forbidden edges, phase boundaries, and waiver semantics.
todos.current_gap: Current dependency gate treats most drift as warnings and lacks policy semantics.
acceptance: Supports forbidden_imports and allowed_imports policy fields
Fails on unwaived forbidden edges
Reports undeclared observed imports separately from forbidden imports
Includes phase/cross-cutting violation checks where struct declares them

todos.task: Rewrite PR impact around base/head diff semantics, component path prefix matching, interface diffs, import fanout, tests, owners, risk, and Markdown output.
todos.current_gap: Current PR impact supports exact file paths and JSON only.
todos.deliverable: pr_impact v2 with JSON and Markdown reports.
acceptance: Accepts --base/--head and current working tree mode
Maps file and directory component paths
Includes interface diff, dependency fanout, selected tests, owners, risk, and waivers
Markdown report is concise enough for PR comments

todos.task: Turn test selection into dry-run and execute modes with command de-duplication, timeouts, exit-code capture, and work-record output.
todos.current_gap: Current test_select only emits commands.
acceptance: Dry-run mode lists commands and reasons
Execute mode captures exit code, duration, stdout/stderr paths
Timeout is configurable and defaults conservatively
Results can be embedded in work records

todos.task: Make risk scoring explainable and calibrated from interface changes, dependency fanout, test coverage, owner gaps, unmanaged code, and waiver count.
todos.current_gap: Current risk_score uses a small heuristic over component risk only.
acceptance: Risk factors are individually listed with points
High-risk factors include removed interfaces and missing tests
Waivers increase risk unless explicitly marked adoption-only
Tests cover low/medium/high/critical classification

todos.task: Route review using owners, CODEOWNERS source, risk level, interface-breaking changes, and orphan escalation.
todos.current_gap: Current review routing returns unique owners only.
todos.deliverable: review_route v2 with required approvals and escalation reasons.
acceptance: Critical risk requires at least two approval candidates when available
Interface-breaking changes include API owner escalation
Orphaned components route to fallback maintainers
Output includes why each reviewer was selected

todos.task: Make work records a real ledger linked to dispatch, slices, commands, tests, changed files, CBOM, and gate verdicts.
todos.current_gap: Current collector only checks minimal required fields and is not integrated into dispatch or CBOM.
todos.deliverable: work record schema v2 and collector/generator integration.
acceptance: Schema requires slice_hash, struct_hash, adapter, started/ended metadata, changed files, commands, tests, and summary
orchestrate_dispatch can create a pending work record stub
orchestrate_collect validates and aggregates work records
CBOM includes work_record_hash

todos.task: Integrate code facts into bounded slices: upstream interfaces, downstream dependents, selected tests, risk, owners, and relevant policies.
todos.current_gap: Current slices are still mostly struct/intent-driven and do not include rich code facts.
acceptance: Slice includes direct upstream public interface signatures
Slice includes downstream components that may break
Slice includes selected tests and owners
Slice size remains bounded with deterministic truncation

todos.task: Wire dispatch adapters to write work record stubs and final summaries under generated/.ai/work_records.
todos.current_gap: Dispatch and work records are separate surfaces.
acceptance: Each dispatched leaf has a deterministic work record path
Adapter failure records status failed/blocked
Collector can aggregate all records for a wave
PR gate can require records for agent-authored changes

todos.task: Rewrite PR gate as the product-grade bundled verdict: validate, impact, facts, interface gate, dependency gate, selected tests, work records, risk, review routing, and waivers.
todos.current_gap: Current pr_gate bundles shallow JSON from a few scripts and does not run tests or waivers.
todos.deliverable: pr_gate v2 with stable exit taxonomy and Markdown report.
acceptance: Stable exit codes distinguish validation, interface, dependency, test, and work-record failures
JSON report includes all child reports and hashes
Markdown report has summary, blockers, warnings, selected tests, and reviewers
Adoption mode can pass with waived known debt but fails on new unwaived debt

todos.task: Generate a production GitHub Actions workflow that posts PR impact/gate reports and uploads artifacts.
todos.current_gap: Current github_action.sh writes a minimal workflow with no PR comment integration.
todos.deliverable: github_action generator v2 and example workflow.
acceptance: Workflow computes base/head refs correctly for pull_request
Runs pr_gate v2 and uploads JSON/Markdown artifacts
Optionally posts or updates a PR comment via gh
Documents required permissions

todos.task: Add a deterministic PR comment renderer for impact/gate/risk/review output.
todos.current_gap: Reports are JSON-first and not easy to consume in code review.
acceptance: Renders compact Markdown from pr_gate JSON
Includes collapsible detail blocks for warnings
Includes reviewer suggestions and selected tests
Has stable marker for comment update workflows

todos.task: Upgrade the MCP wrapper into a small compliant stdio tool server with method discovery, schemas, errors, and read-only operations.
todos.current_gap: Current MCP wrapper handles a few one-shot methods and is not a compliant tool server.
acceptance: Supports initialize, tools/list, tools/call style methods
Exposes resolved_struct, component_explain, code_facts, pr_impact, pr_gate, next_task
Returns structured JSON-RPC errors
Tests cover at least initialize/list/call/error

todos.task: Make multi-repo federation resolve cross-repo component ids, interfaces, imports, owners, and downstream PR impact.
todos.current_gap: Current federation_resolve reads multiple structs but does not build cross-repo facts.
acceptance: Federation spec supports repo id, path, struct, default branch, and owner
Resolved component ids are globally unique
Cross-repo imports are represented as facts
PR impact can report downstream cross-repo consumers

todos.task: Plan large-scale migrations into dependency-safe PR batches with owners, tests, risk caps, and gate expectations.
todos.current_gap: Current batch_plan places impacted components into one trivial batch.
acceptance: Accepts component list or PR impact report
Batches respect dependency topological order
Batch size and risk cap are configurable
Each batch includes owners, tests, expected gates, and rationale

todos.task: Add a benchmark fixture or synthetic generator for 1k/10k/100k file projects to measure scan time, cache hits, and report size.
todos.current_gap: Current tests are tiny fixtures and do not prove large-project behavior.
acceptance: Generates synthetic repo fixtures without external dependencies
Measures cold scan, warm scan, and one-file-change scan
Outputs JSON metrics
Has conservative thresholds documented but not brittle

todos.task: Turn architecture debt into a ranked, actionable report with finding ids, owners, suggested struct patches, waivers, and trend hashes.
todos.current_gap: Current architecture_debt.sh aggregates counts only.
acceptance: Every finding has stable id, severity, component/path, owner, rule, and suggested remediation
Report includes top risks and adoption progress
Supports baseline comparison against previous report
Markdown report is readable without jq

todos.task: Generate a static HTML dashboard for project intelligence: coverage, interfaces, imports, tests, owners, debt, gates, and trends.
todos.current_gap: The project has architecture visualization but not an operational maintenance dashboard.
acceptance: Single-file HTML output under generated/dashboard.html
No external network assets
Includes sortable tables for findings and components
Links back to source paths and reports

todos.task: Write a concrete adoption playbook for a half-built large project: ingest, split struct, scan facts, fix drift, add waivers, enable PR gate.
todos.current_gap: The commands exist but the adoption path is not yet operationally clear.
acceptance: Includes 30-minute, 1-day, and 1-week adoption paths
Shows multi-file struct examples
Explains adoption mode vs enforcement mode
Includes troubleshooting for noisy scans

todos.task: Replace the smoke evolution harness with real multi-step scenarios measuring drift, gate quality, work records, and fact cache behavior.
todos.current_gap: Current evolution harness is a static three-step smoke JSON.
acceptance: Runs at least three sequential repo mutations
Measures whether gates catch introduced interface/dependency/test drift
Measures fact cache invalidation across steps
Outputs JSON metrics and Markdown summary

todos.task: Evaluate agent usefulness with bounded slices and work records across Claude/Codex/generic adapters on deterministic fixtures.
todos.current_gap: The project can dispatch adapters but does not measure whether code-fact slices improve outcomes.
acceptance: Runs generic adapter without network
Can optionally run real adapters when env vars are set
Compares slice size and included facts
Validates returned work records and gate results

todos.task: Generate release-facing contract reports: public interface changes, migration files, downstream impact, and required acknowledgements.
todos.current_gap: Interface changes are internal gate data, not release-facing artifacts.
acceptance: Consumes interface diff and PR impact
Separates breaking, additive, and internal-only changes
Includes migration/config/env changes
Outputs Markdown suitable for release notes

non_goals: Do not build a new general-purpose autonomous coding agent.
Do not require hosted services, vector databases, or ML models for core scans and gates.
Do not claim production-grade precision from regex scanners without fixtures and clear confidence metadata.
Do not make PR gates noisy without waiver and adoption-mode controls.
Do not add more surfaces before hardening the existing fact/gate/report loop.
